blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: My parents sat me down Thursday night. I already had your hand in my head from Wednesday. I was already thinking about what I was going to say the next time I saw you. And then they told me.
I didn't say anything. I couldn't say anything.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: My parents always taught me that love is serious. That it's not something you throw around or waste. And I don't. I never do.
She was speaking slowly now. Not hesitating — choosing. Every word deliberate.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I wanted you to be that person for me.
The apartment was gone. The bed was gone. I was just on a phone in the dark and she was on steps in Atlanta and nothing else existed for a second.
I wanted you to be that person for me.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm scared you're going to find someone — someone who's actually there — and I'm going to be in Atlanta trying to make peace with it from a thousand miles away. I've been sitting on these steps for twenty minutes trying to make peace with it and I just —
She couldn't finish the sentence. I heard her breathing change — she was trying to hold something back and it was bigger than the trying.
The phone moved away from her face. I could still hear the night around her. I waited.
I don't know how long I waited. Long enough that I was afraid. Then she came back.
